key_insight: Gradient clipping makes asynchronous SGD robust to slow workers by removing the oracle-complexity dependence on the maximum delay.
engineering_relevance: For distributed and federated training, clipping can stabilize asynchronous optimization under stragglers and heavy-tailed gradient noise without sacrificing scalability.
method: Model gradient noise with a sub-Weibull distribution to cover heavy-tailed behavior.
Analyze asynchronous SGD with constant step sizes under gradient clipping.
Prove convergence in expectation and high-probability convergence, with delay-independence in the clipped setting.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence results, including the first high-probability guarantee for asynchronous optimization under the stated noise model.
watch_item: Clipping may reduce sensitivity to stragglers, but its threshold must be tuned carefully because overly aggressive clipping can bias updates or slow learning.
paper_title: Clipping Makes Distributed and Federated Asynchronous SGD Robust to Stragglers
